title: vldaft — Project Instructions
text: vldaft implements Anderson (1991) Varying Location Dispersion AFT model. core computation Newton-Raphson maximization log-likelihood modified Cholesky decomposition, implemented C Rust backends.

title: vldaft — Project Instructions
text: R/vldaft.R — main fitting function vldaft(), formula parsing, dispatch C Rust R/methods.R — S3 methods: print, summary, coef, vcov, logLik R/family_aft.R — gamlss2 family constructors (AFT_Weibull, etc.) src/*.c — C backend (Newton-Raphson, distributions, matrix algebra, data /O) src/rust/src/ — Rust backend via extendr (algorithms, f64 precision) src/init.c — R routine registration C Rust entry points data/ — Framingham Heart Study datasets (.rda) vignettes/anderson1991.Rmd — reproduces models 1991 paper tests/testthat/ — 92 unit tests covering paper results, backend parity, methods, edge cases

title: vldaft — Project Instructions
text: Log-likelihood log-time scale (Jacobian). compare survreg: vldaft_ll + sum(d * log(t)) ≈ survreg_ll Coefficient names use prefixes: gamma: (location), eta: (scale covariates), theta: (coupling) backend argument selects "c" "rust" via .Call() dispatch C code uses float storage MAXCOV=30, MAXSS=210 limits; Rust uses f64 throughout Packed symmetric (lower-triangle) matrix storage Hessian/covariance

title: vldaft — Project Instructions
text: src/Makevars compiles C sources runs cargo build --release Rust static library Rust source src/rust/ Cargo.toml; uses extendr-api 0.7 libm 0.2 System requirements: R ≥ 3.5, Rust toolchain (rustc + cargo)

title: vldaft — Project Instructions
text: Run tests: devtools::test() testthat::test_local() 92 tests pass; C Rust backends match ~1e-7 precision Paper log-likelihoods: Model 1 = -2510.96, Model 2 = -2504.40, Model 3b = -2496.65

title: vldaft — Project Instructions
text: changing C backend, mirror change corresponding Rust module changing R interface arguments, update C call (vldaft_fit) Rust call (wrap__vldaft_fit_rust) R/vldaft.R registration src/init.c changes, rebuild R CMD INSTALL . run devtools::test()

title: Reproducing Anderson (1991): A Nonproportional Hazards Weibull AFT Model
text: Anderson (1991) introduced varying location dispersion accelerated failure time (VLDAFT) model, location μ\mu dispersion σ\sigma log-linear survival model can depend covariates . vignette reproduces results original Biometrics paper using vldaft package. done original C routines compute log-likelihood score functions, well gamlss2 framework comparison. approaches yield results replicate paper. hope demonstration enables use approach can simplify modeling. particular advantage Weibull distribution, VLDAFT model allows nonproportional hazards letting dispersion depend location parameter. Reference: Anderson, K.M. (1991). nonproportional hazards Weibull accelerated failure time regression model. Biometrics, 47, 281–288.

title: Reproducing Anderson (1991): A Nonproportional Hazards Weibull AFT Model
text: VLDAFT model assumes Pr{log(T)−μσ<u}=F(u),\Pr\!\left\{\frac{\log(T) - \mu}{\sigma} < u\right\} = F(u), μ=𝛃′𝐗\mu = \boldsymbol{\beta}'\mathbf{X} linear function covariates, log-dispersion log(σ)\log(\sigma) can depend covariates μ\mu : log(σ)=θ0+θ1μ*.\log(\sigma) = \theta_0 + \theta_1 \mu^*. μ*=μ−μ‾\mu^* = \mu - \bar\mu centered location parameter. θ1=0\theta_1 = 0, reduces standard linear location AFT (LLAFT) model proportional hazards Weibull distribution.

title: Reproducing Anderson (1991): A Nonproportional Hazards Weibull AFT Model
text: example uses 1,433 men aged 30–49 Framingham Heart Study, followed approximately 32 years coronary heart disease (CHD) incidence. Risk factors measured baseline. covariates log(Age), log(systolic blood pressure), log(cholesterol), Metropolitan relative weight, smoking indicator.
code:
cat("Observations:", info$observations, "
") #> Observations: 1433 cat("CHD events:", info$chd_events, "
") #> CHD events: 501 cat("Censored:", info$censored, "
") #> Censored: 932 print(info$covariate_summary) #>     ln.Age.         ln.SBP.         ln.Chol.          MRW        #>  Min.   :3.401   Min.   :4.382   Min.   :4.745   Min.   : 78.0   #>  1st Qu.:3.584   1st Qu.:4.771   1st Qu.:5.288   1st Qu.:108.0   #>  Median :3.689   Median :4.852   Median :5.398   Median :118.0   #>  Mean   :3.694   Mean   :4.858   Mean   :5.398   Mean   :119.2   #>  3rd Qu.:3.807   3rd Qu.:4.942   3rd Qu.:5.509   3rd Qu.:130.0   #>  Max.   :3.892   Max.   :5.521   Max.   :6.342   Max.   :183.0   #>      Smoke        #>  Min.   :0.0000   #>  1st Qu.:0.0000   #>  Median :1.0000   #>  Mean   :0.6971   #>  3rd Qu.:1.0000   #>  Max.   :1.0000

title: Reproducing Anderson (1991): A Nonproportional Hazards Weibull AFT Model
text: Anderson (1991) reported four Weibull models increasing complexity. reproduce four.

title: Reproducing Anderson (1991): A Nonproportional Hazards Weibull AFT Model
text: μ=𝛃′𝐗,log(σ)=θ0\mu = \boldsymbol{\beta}'\mathbf{X}, \quad \log(\sigma) = \theta_0 Since Model 1 standard Weibull AFT, can cross-check survival::survreg: coefficients scale parameter agree, confirming vldaft implementation standard model.
code:
fit1 <- vldaft(Surv(YTCHD, CHD) ~ ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. + MRW + Smoke,                data = MLT50, dist = "weibull", theta = 0) cat(paste(summaries$fit1, collapse = "
"), "
") #> AFT Regression Model (Anderson 1991) #>  #> Call: #> vldaft(formula = Surv(YTCHD, CHD) ~ ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. +  #>     MRW + Smoke, data = MLT50, dist = "weibull", theta = 0) #>  #> Distribution: weibull  #> Observations: 1433  #> Log-likelihood: -1104.69  (init: -35508.1 ) #> Score statistic: -1  #> Iterations: 14  #>  #> Coefficients: #>                     Estimate Std. Error  z value  Pr(>|z|)     #> gamma:(Intercept) -0.5804654  0.0401405 -14.4608 < 2.2e-16 *** #> eta:(Intercept)    3.9189608  0.0340533 115.0832 < 2.2e-16 *** #> eta:ln.Age.       -1.2221808  0.2162649  -5.6513 1.592e-08 *** #> eta:ln.SBP.       -0.9485825  0.2070646  -4.5811 4.625e-06 *** #> eta:ln.Chol.      -0.9503949  0.1415783  -6.7129 1.908e-11 *** #> eta:MRW           -0.0046964  0.0016137  -2.9102 0.0036114 **  #> eta:Smoke         -0.1989825  0.0565299  -3.5199 0.0004316 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 fit_survreg <- survreg(   Surv(YTCHD, CHD) ~ ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. + MRW + Smoke,   data = MLT50, dist = "weibull") print(checks$coef_compare, digits = 7) #>                  survreg       vldaft    difference #> (Intercept) 18.869878310 18.869878192  1.184292e-07 #> ln.Age.     -1.222180634 -1.222180769  1.350743e-07 #> ln.SBP.     -0.948582322 -0.948582481  1.582619e-07 #> ln.Chol.    -0.950395178 -0.950394935 -2.432144e-07 #> MRW         -0.004696388 -0.004696388 -1.468580e-10 #> Smoke       -0.198982499 -0.198982501  2.195279e-09 # Compare scale parameter cat("survreg scale (sigma):", checks$survreg_sigma, "
") #> survreg scale (sigma): 0.5596379 cat("vldaft  scale (sigma):", checks$vldaft_sigma, "
") #> vldaft  scale (sigma): 0.5596379

what: Model 2: VLDAFT with dispersion depending on location
title: Reproducing Anderson (1991): A Nonproportional Hazards Weibull AFT Model
text: μ=𝛃′𝐗,log(σ)=θ0+θ1μ*\mu = \boldsymbol{\beta}'\mathbf{X}, \quad \log(\sigma) = \theta_0 + \theta_1 \mu^*
code: fit2 <- vldaft(Surv(YTCHD, CHD) ~ ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. + MRW + Smoke,                data = MLT50, dist = "weibull", theta = 1) cat(paste(summaries$fit2, collapse = "\n"), "\n") #> AFT Regression Model (Anderson 1991) #>  #> Call: #> vldaft(formula = Surv(YTCHD, CHD) ~ ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. +  #>     MRW + Smoke, data = MLT50, dist = "weibull", theta = 1) #>  #> Distribution: weibull  #> Observations: 1433  #> Log-likelihood: -1094.24  (init: -35508.1 ) #> Score statistic: -1  #> Iterations: 14  #>  #> Coefficients: #>                     Estimate Std. Error  z value  Pr(>|z|)     #> gamma:(Intercept)  2.9778006  1.3457787   2.2127 0.0269185 *   #> eta:(Intercept)    3.8385302  0.0321886 119.2511 < 2.2e-16 *** #> eta:ln.Age.       -0.7417504  0.1770810  -4.1888 2.805e-05 *** #> eta:ln.SBP.       -0.6579658  0.1730218  -3.8028 0.0001431 *** #> eta:ln.Chol.      -0.6071953  0.1341263  -4.5270 5.982e-06 *** #> eta:MRW           -0.0026011  0.0011570  -2.2481 0.0245711 *   #> eta:Smoke         -0.1594967  0.0389560  -4.0943 4.235e-05 *** #> theta1            -0.9525121  0.3585544  -2.6565 0.0078948 **  #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

title: Reproducing Anderson (1991): A Nonproportional Hazards Weibull AFT Model
text: μ=𝛃′𝐗,log(σ)=θ0+θ1μ*+θ2μ*2\mu = \boldsymbol{\beta}'\mathbf{X}, \quad \log(\sigma) = \theta_0 + \theta_1 \mu^* + \theta_2 {\mu^*}^2 noted paper, convergence zero starting values can difficult complex models. use Model 2 estimates starting values, appending zero new θ2\theta_2 parameter.
code: init3a <- c(fit2$coefficients, 0) fit3a <- vldaft(Surv(YTCHD, CHD) ~ ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. + MRW + Smoke,                 data = MLT50, dist = "weibull", theta = 2, init = init3a) cat(paste(summaries$fit3a, collapse = "\n"), "\n") #> AFT Regression Model (Anderson 1991) #>  #> Call: #> vldaft(formula = Surv(YTCHD, CHD) ~ ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. +  #>     MRW + Smoke, data = MLT50, dist = "weibull", theta = 2, init = init3a) #>  #> Distribution: weibull  #> Observations: 1433  #> Log-likelihood: -1094.2  (init: -1094.24 ) #> Score statistic: 0.289811  #> Iterations: 4  #>  #> Coefficients: #>                     Estimate Std. Error  z value  Pr(>|z|)     #> gamma:(Intercept)  0.5870780  8.1632058   0.0719 0.9426675     #> eta:(Intercept)    3.8377582  0.0322726 118.9168 < 2.2e-16 *** #> eta:ln.Age.       -0.7227241  0.1862179  -3.8811 0.0001040 *** #> eta:ln.SBP.       -0.6569947  0.1720719  -3.8181 0.0001345 *** #> eta:ln.Chol.      -0.6017253  0.1353597  -4.4454 8.774e-06 *** #> eta:MRW           -0.0025126  0.0011802  -2.1290 0.0332502 *   #> eta:Smoke         -0.1574287  0.0391718  -4.0189 5.846e-05 *** #> theta1             0.3278279  4.3631493   0.0751 0.9401068     #> theta2            -0.1709261  0.5854120  -0.2920 0.7703052     #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

title: Reproducing Anderson (1991): A Nonproportional Hazards Weibull AFT Model
text: μ=𝛃′𝐗,log(σ)=𝛄′𝐗\mu = \boldsymbol{\beta}'\mathbf{X}, \quad \log(\sigma) = \boldsymbol{\gamma}'\mathbf{X} location scale separate linear predictors full set covariates. | operator formula separates location (left) scale (right) covariates.
code: fit3b <- vldaft(   Surv(YTCHD, CHD) ~ ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. + MRW + Smoke |                       ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. + MRW + Smoke,   data = MLT50, dist = "weibull", theta = 0) cat(paste(summaries$fit3b, collapse = "\n"), "\n") #> AFT Regression Model (Anderson 1991) #>  #> Call: #> vldaft(formula = Surv(YTCHD, CHD) ~ ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. +  #>     MRW + Smoke | ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. + MRW + Smoke,  #>     data = MLT50, dist = "weibull", theta = 0) #>  #> Distribution: weibull  #> Observations: 1433  #> Log-likelihood: -1092.5  (init: -35508.1 ) #> Score statistic: -1  #> Iterations: 18  #>  #> Coefficients: #>                     Estimate Std. Error  z value  Pr(>|z|)     #> gamma:(Intercept) -0.6740649  0.0442573 -15.2306 < 2.2e-16 *** #> gamma:ln.Age.      0.7035408  0.3348376   2.1011 0.0356286 *   #> gamma:ln.SBP.      0.5379124  0.3174538   1.6945 0.0901782 .   #> gamma:ln.Chol.     0.4767665  0.2323611   2.0518 0.0401858 *   #> gamma:MRW          0.0014924  0.0025257   0.5909 0.5545905     #> gamma:Smoke        0.2940132  0.0912093   3.2235 0.0012663 **  #> eta:(Intercept)    3.8446356  0.0330466 116.3399 < 2.2e-16 *** #> eta:ln.Age.       -0.7525451  0.2549722  -2.9515 0.0031626 **  #> eta:ln.SBP.       -0.7599684  0.2354839  -3.2273 0.0012498 **  #> eta:ln.Chol.      -0.6658450  0.1720855  -3.8693 0.0001092 *** #> eta:MRW           -0.0032470  0.0018402  -1.7645 0.0776476 .   #> eta:Smoke         -0.0688494  0.0619879  -1.1107 0.2667015     #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

title: Reproducing Anderson (1991): A Nonproportional Hazards Weibull AFT Model
text: paper reports log-likelihoods time scale, includes Jacobian term ∑dilog(ti)\sum d_i \log(t_i). adjust log-likelihoods accordingly.
code: print(checks$results_table, row.names = FALSE) #>                 Model   vldaft    Paper Difference #>             1 (LLAFT) -2510.96 -2510.96          0 #>   2 (VLDAFT, theta=1) -2500.50 -2500.50          0 #>  3a (VLDAFT, theta=2) -2500.46 -2500.46          0 #>       3b (full scale) -2498.76 -2498.76          0

what: Likelihood Ratio Test: Model 2 vs Model 1
title: Reproducing Anderson (1991): A Nonproportional Hazards Weibull AFT Model
text: LLAFT model strongly rejected favor VLDAFT model (χ12=20.9\chi^2_1 = 20.9, p<0.001p < 0.001).
code: cat(sprintf("LRT statistic: %.2f  (paper: 20.92)\n", checks$lrt)) #> LRT statistic: 20.91  (paper: 20.92) cat(sprintf("p-value (chi-sq, 1 df): %.2e\n", checks$lrt_p)) #> p-value (chi-sq, 1 df): 4.83e-06

title: Reproducing Anderson (1991): A Nonproportional Hazards Weibull AFT Model
text: coefficients match published values reported precision.
code: print(checks$coef_table, row.names = FALSE) #>           Parameter  vldaft     Paper #>   beta0 (intercept) 13.4733 13.474000 #>      beta1 (ln Age) -0.7418 -0.741800 #>      beta2 (ln SBP) -0.6580 -0.658000 #>     beta3 (ln Chol) -0.6072 -0.607200 #>         beta4 (MRW) -0.0026 -0.002601 #>       beta5 (Smoke) -0.1595 -0.159500 #>  theta0 (log sigma)  2.9778  2.978000 #>              theta1 -0.9525 -0.952500

title: Reproducing Anderson (1991): A Nonproportional Hazards Weibull AFT Model
text: vldaft package provides gamlss2-compatible family objects (AFT_Weibull, AFT_Logistic, etc.) models can fitted gamlss2 framework. enables use smooth terms, additional diagnostics, full gamlss2 infrastructure. demonstrate fitting Model 2 (VLDAFT model θ1\theta_1 coupling) via gamlss2. gamlss2 formula, | separator delineates submodels parameter: mu | sigma | theta1. gamlss2 log-likelihood matches vldaft() result: can also verify Models 1, 3a, 3b agree:
code: library(gamlss2)  g2 <- gamlss2(   Surv(YTCHD, CHD) ~ ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. + MRW + Smoke | 1 | 1,   data = MLT50, family = AFT_Weibull(theta = 1),   control = gamlss2_control(maxit = 200)) summary(g2) cat(sprintf("vldaft  loglik: %.3f\ngamlss2 loglik: %.3f\n",             checks$gamlss2_loglik[["vldaft"]],             checks$gamlss2_loglik[["gamlss2"]])) #> vldaft  loglik: -2500.503 #> gamlss2 loglik: -2500.510 g1 <- gamlss2(   Surv(YTCHD, CHD) ~ ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. + MRW + Smoke,   data = MLT50, family = AFT_Weibull())  g3a <- gamlss2(   Surv(YTCHD, CHD) ~ ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. + MRW + Smoke | 1 | 1 | 1,   data = MLT50, family = AFT_Weibull(theta = 2),   control = gamlss2_control(maxit = 200))  g3b <- gamlss2(   Surv(YTCHD, CHD) ~ ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. + MRW + Smoke |                       ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. + MRW + Smoke,   data = MLT50, family = AFT_Weibull()) print(checks$gamlss2_check, row.names = FALSE) #>                 Model   vldaft  gamlss2 Difference #>             1 (LLAFT) -2510.96 -2510.96       0.00 #>   2 (VLDAFT, theta=1) -2500.50 -2500.51      -0.01 #>  3a (VLDAFT, theta=2) -2500.46 -2500.47      -0.01 #>       3b (full scale) -2498.76 -2498.76       0.00

title: Reproducing Anderson (1991): A Nonproportional Hazards Weibull AFT Model
text: significant negative θ1=−0.95\theta_1 = -0.95 means μ\mu increases (.e., predicted log-survival time increases due lower risk), dispersion σ\sigma also increases. Equivalently, cumulative hazard ratio two individuals shrinks toward unity longer follow-. implies: Neither standard Weibull model Cox proportional hazards model adequate data. association risk factors relative risk diminishes time since measurement—“converging hazards” phenomenon.

title: Reproducing Anderson (1991): A Nonproportional Hazards Weibull AFT Model
text: Follow-dataset ranges less 1 month nearly 36 years (median 31 years, interquartile range 19–34 years). restrict predictions 30 years stay within well-observed range. illustrate nonproportional hazards effect, compare predicted cumulative incidence F(t)=1−S(t)F(t) = 1 - S(t) two individuals differing total cholesterol (160 vs 240 mg/dL), covariates held sample means. Weibull model, S(t)=exp{−exp(w)}S(t) = \exp\{-\exp(w)\} w=(logt−μ)/σw = (\log t - \mu)/\sigma.  difference cumulative incidence cholesterol 240 160 quantifies much additional risk higher cholesterol confers time model.  models, higher cholesterol associated higher cumulative incidence CHD time. difference cumulative incidence also grows time. observation difference always greater VLDAFT model reflects model’s ability capture nonproportional hazards.
code: t_grid <- prediction$t_grid inc1_240 <- prediction$incidence$model1_chol240 inc1_160 <- prediction$incidence$model1_chol160 inc2_240 <- prediction$incidence$model2_chol240 inc2_160 <- prediction$incidence$model2_chol160 diff1 <- prediction$excess$model1 diff2 <- prediction$excess$model2 plot(t_grid, inc1_240, type = "l",      col = "firebrick", lwd = 2, lty = 1, ylim = c(0, 0.6),      xlab = "Years since baseline", ylab = "Cumulative CHD incidence",      main = "Predicted Incidence: Cholesterol 240 vs 160") lines(t_grid, inc1_160,       col = "steelblue", lwd = 2, lty = 1) lines(t_grid, inc2_240,       col = "firebrick", lwd = 2, lty = 2) lines(t_grid, inc2_160,       col = "steelblue", lwd = 2, lty = 2) legend("topleft",        legend = c("Chol = 240, Model 1 (LLAFT)",                   "Chol = 160, Model 1 (LLAFT)",                   "Chol = 240, Model 2 (VLDAFT)",                   "Chol = 160, Model 2 (VLDAFT)"),        col = c("firebrick", "steelblue", "firebrick", "steelblue"),        lty = c(1, 1, 2, 2), lwd = 2, bty = "n", cex = 0.85) plot(t_grid, diff1, type = "l", lwd = 2, lty = 1,      col = "black", ylim = c(0, max(diff1, diff2) * 1.1),      xlab = "Years since baseline",      ylab = "Difference in cumulative incidence",      main = "Excess Incidence: Cholesterol 240 vs 160") lines(t_grid, diff2, lwd = 2, lty = 2, col = "black") legend("topleft",        legend = c("Model 1 (LLAFT)", "Model 2 (VLDAFT)"),        lty = c(1, 2), lwd = 2, bty = "n")

title: Survival Distribution Explorer
text: interactive vignette lets explore five survival distributions vldaft accelerated failure time (AFT) log-time scale. can compare families matched 25th 75th percentiles, compare multiple parameter sets within single family. Toggle CDF, PDF, hazard rate. AFT parameterization: w=(logt−μ)/σw = (\log t - \mu) / \sigma. curves computed standardized error distribution ww, mapped time tt.

title: vldaft — Copilot Instructions
text: vldaft implements Anderson (1991) Varying Location Dispersion AFT model. core computation Newton-Raphson maximization log-likelihood modified Cholesky decomposition, implemented C Rust backends.

title: vldaft — Copilot Instructions
text: R/vldaft.R — main fitting function vldaft(), formula parsing, dispatch C Rust R/methods.R — S3 methods: print, summary, coef, vcov, logLik R/family_aft.R — gamlss2 family constructors (AFT_Weibull, etc.) src/*.c — C backend (Newton-Raphson, distributions, matrix algebra, data /O) src/rust/src/ — Rust backend via extendr (algorithms, f64 precision) src/init.c — R routine registration C Rust entry points data/ — Framingham Heart Study datasets (.rda) vignettes/anderson1991.Rmd — reproduces models 1991 paper tests/testthat/ — 92 unit tests covering paper results, backend parity, methods, edge cases

title: vldaft — Copilot Instructions
text: Log-likelihood log-time scale (Jacobian). compare survreg: vldaft_ll + sum(d * log(t)) ≈ survreg_ll Coefficient names use prefixes: gamma: (location), eta: (scale covariates), theta: (coupling) backend argument selects "c" "rust" via .Call() dispatch C code uses float storage MAXCOV=30, MAXSS=210 limits; Rust uses f64 throughout Packed symmetric (lower-triangle) matrix storage Hessian/covariance

title: vldaft — Copilot Instructions
text: src/Makevars compiles C sources runs cargo build --release Rust static library Rust source src/rust/ Cargo.toml; uses extendr-api 0.7 libm 0.2 System requirements: R ≥ 3.5, Rust toolchain (rustc + cargo)

title: vldaft — Copilot Instructions
text: Run tests: devtools::test() testthat::test_local() 92 tests pass; C Rust backends match ~1e-7 precision Paper log-likelihoods: Model 1 = -2510.96, Model 2 = -2504.40, Model 3b = -2496.65

title: vldaft — Copilot Instructions
text: changing C backend, mirror change corresponding Rust module changing R interface arguments, update C call (vldaft_fit) Rust call (wrap__vldaft_fit_rust) R/vldaft.R registration src/init.c changes, rebuild R CMD INSTALL . run devtools::test()

what: Non-Proportional Hazards Accelerated Failure Time Regression
title: Non-Proportional Hazards Accelerated Failure Time Regression
text: Varying Location Dispersion Accelerated Failure Time (VLDAFT) regression, implementing model Anderson (1991). location (μ) dispersion (σ) log-survival time can depend covariates, dispersion can depend fitted location theta polynomial coupling — enabling nonproportional hazards Weibull parametric distributions. C programming supporting original 1991 manuscript updated standards required R. Either C Rust backends can used computattions.

title: Non-Proportional Hazards Accelerated Failure Time Regression
Non-Proportional Hazards Accelerated Failure Time Regression
text: Rust toolchain (rustc + cargo) required. Install https://rustup.rs.

title: Non-Proportional Hazards Accelerated Failure Time Regression
code: library(vldaft) library(survival) data(MLT50)  # Standard Weibull AFT (constant scale) fit1 <- vldaft(Surv(YTCHD, CHD) ~ ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. + MRW + Smoke,                data = MLT50, dist = "weibull", theta = 0)  # Scale coupled to location via theta polynomial fit2 <- vldaft(Surv(YTCHD, CHD) ~ ln.Age. + ln.SBP. + ln.Chol. + MRW + Smoke,                data = MLT50, dist = "weibull", theta = 1)  summary(fit2)

title: Non-Proportional Hazards Accelerated Failure Time Regression
text: Five error distributions: Weibull, logistic, normal, Cauchy, gamma Censoring: right censoring, left censoring, left truncation Scale–location coupling: polynomial theta parameters linking σ μ Scale covariates: separate covariates scale (dispersion) model via theta_vars Dual backends: C Rust implementations selectable via backend = c("c", "rust") gamlss2 families: AFT_Weibull(), AFT_Logistic(), AFT_Normal(), AFT_Cauchy(), AFT_Gamma() use gamlss2 framework Datasets: Framingham Heart Study subsets Anderson (1991)

title: Non-Proportional Hazards Accelerated Failure Time Regression
text: Anderson, K. M. (1991). nonproportional hazards Weibull accelerated failure time regression model. Biometrics, 47(1), 281–288.

title: AFT Family Constructors for gamlss2 — AFT_families
text: gamlss2.family object.

title: AFT Family Constructors for gamlss2 — AFT_families
text: model : $$\log(T) = \mu + \sigma_{eff} \cdot \varepsilon$$ \(\log(\sigma_{eff}) = \log(\sigma) + \sum_k \theta_k (\mu - \bar\mu)^k\) Parameters: mu Location parameter (identity link) sigma Scale parameter (log link) theta1, theta2, ... Coupling parameters (identity link), theta > 0

what: Fit an Accelerated Failure Time Model with Non-Proportional Hazards — vldaft
title: Fit an Accelerated Failure Time Model with Non-Proportional Hazards — vldaft
text: Fits Anderson (1991) AFT regression model location (mu) scale (sigma) functions covariates, sigma can depend mu theta polynomial.

title: Fit an Accelerated Failure Time Model with Non-Proportional Hazards — vldaft
code: vldaft(   formula,   data,   dist = c("weibull", "logistic", "normal", "cauchy", "gamma"),   theta = 0L,   theta_vars = NULL,   nu = 1,   init = NULL,   control = list(),   adjust = TRUE,   backend = c("c", "rust") )

title: Fit an Accelerated Failure Time Model with Non-Proportional Hazards — vldaft
text: formula formula form Surv(time, status) ~ loc_vars | scale_vars. Left | specifies location (mu) covariates, right specifies scale (gamma) covariates. | present, covariates enter location model scale intercept . data data frame. dist Character string specifying error distribution: "weibull" (default), "logistic", "normal", "cauchy", "gamma". theta Integer, order theta polynomial linking log(sigma) mu. 0 = coupling (default), 1 = linear mu, 2 = quadratic, etc. theta_vars one-sided formula specifying location covariates form mu* (mu feeds theta polynomial sigma). Default NULL means location covariates. nu Numeric, shape parameter gamma distribution (used dist = "gamma"). init Numeric vector initial parameter values, NULL zeros. control list control parameters. See Details. adjust Logical, whether center covariates (default TRUE). backend Character, computation backend use: "c" (default) "rust".

title: Fit an Accelerated Failure Time Model with Non-Proportional Hazards — vldaft
text: object class "vldaft".

title: Fit an Accelerated Failure Time Model with Non-Proportional Hazards — vldaft
text: model assumes $$\log(T) = \mu + \sigma \cdot \varepsilon$$ location linear function covariates, $$\mu = \beta_0 + \beta_1 x_1 + \cdots + \beta_p x_p$$ log-dispersion can depend covariates location: $$\log(\sigma) = \gamma_0 + \gamma_1 z_1 + \cdots + \gamma_q z_q + \theta_1 \mu^{*} + \theta_2 \mu^{*2} + \cdots$$ \(\mu^{*} = \mu - \bar{\mu}\) centered location. \(\theta_1 = 0\), reduces standard linear location AFT model proportional hazards Weibull distribution. error distribution \(F\) can Weibull (extreme value), logistic, normal, Cauchy, gamma. Control parameters: acc Convergence accuracy (default 0.0001) maxiter Maximum Newton-Raphson iterations (default 50) maxhalv Maximum step halvings (default 20)

title: Fit an Accelerated Failure Time Model with Non-Proportional Hazards — vldaft
text: Anderson, K.M. (1991). nonproportional hazards Weibull accelerated failure time regression model. Biometrics, 47, 281-288.
